parameters.query: SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(s) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, project_id::text AS spec_project_id, status, title, requirements, acceptance_criteria, constraints, dependencies, non_goals, summary, body_markdown FROM agent_specifications WHERE id = $1::uuid) s) AS spec;

SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT id::text AS id, version, status, request_payload_hash, stored_response FROM agent_architectures WHERE project_id = $1::uuid AND spec_id = $2::uuid AND action = $3::text AND idempotency_key = $4::text LIMIT 1) a) AS hit;
SELECT (SELECT row_to_json(a) FROM (SELECT version, status FROM agent_architectures WHERE id = $1::uuid LIMIT 1) a) AS row;
INSERT INTO agent_memories (agent_name, memory_type, content, metadata) VALUES ('architecture_agent', 'decision', $1::text, $2::jsonb) RETURNING id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ [ ('Architecture ' + $json.result.architecture.id + ' v' + $json.result.architecture.version + ' for project ' + $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id + ' spec ' + $('Validate Input').first().json.spec_id + ': ' + (($('Parse + Validate Architecture').first().json.parsed.summary || 'no summary').slice(0, 200))), JSON.stringify({ architecture_id: $json.result.architecture.id, spec_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.spec_id, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, version: $json.result.architecture.version, action: $('Validate Input').first().json.action, task_count: ($('Parse + Validate Architecture').first().json.parsed.tasks || []).length, component_count: ($('Parse + Validate Architecture').first().json.parsed.components || []).length, idempotency_key_present: true }) ] }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ ({ source: 'architecture_agent', action: 'architecture_written', architecture_id: $('Create Architecture Revision').first().json.result.architecture.id, project_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.project_id, spec_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.spec_id, version: $('Create Architecture Revision').first().json.result.architecture.version, status: $('Create Architecture Revision').first().json.result.architecture.status, task_count: ($('Parse + Validate Architecture').first().json.parsed.tasks || []).length, component_count: ($('Parse + Validate Architecture').first().json.parsed.components || []).length }) }}

parameters.responseBody: ={{ ({ error: 'internal_error', message: 'database unavailable: create_architecture_revision failed', details: null, request_id: $('Validate Input').first().json.request_id }) }}

meta.version_note: v1 — initial build mirroring AGEN-WKFL-spec-workflow-v1 contract patterns (auth, validation, idempotency safe-replay + conflict, RPC error mapping, error envelope, DiagnosticTicket gap tracked under task f9aa8008). Closes codex pass 1 findings AGEN-ARCH-001 (postgres SELECTs wrapped in row_to_json subquery so they always emit one item), AGEN-ARCH-002 (Load Prior Architecture node feeds prior architecture into Build Prompt for refine), AGEN-ARCH-003 (Anthropic jsonBody passes object expression rather than JSON.stringify). Closes codex pass 2 findings AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-001 (Write Memory onError moved to top-level node property) and AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-002 (Validate Input uses Web Crypto API instead of require('crypto'), portable without NODE_FUNCTION_ALLOW_BUILTIN). Closes codex pass 3 finding AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-CRIT-001 (every node with onError=continueErrorOutput now exposes its failure path as connections[node].main[1], n8n's canonical second-main-output convention — previous error key was nonstandard and could have left webhook calls hanging on DB / Anthropic failures). Closes codex pass 4 ship-gate finding AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-001 (removed hardcoded LAN URL fallback from Log to Document Control — APEX_DOC_CONTROL_URL env var is now required; all RespondToWebhook nodes and the Log to Document Control jsonBody pass object expressions instead of JSON.stringify(...) to avoid double-stringification when respondWith/specifyBody is 'json'). Closes codex pass 5 ship-gate findings AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-001 (Respond Upstream/Val Error now maps to the canonical {error,message,details,request_id} envelope and never leaks the internal http_status field into the response body — HTTP status is still selected via options.responseCode) and AGEN-ARCH-SHIP-002 (new Reload Replayed Row postgres node reads version + status by architecture_id after an RPC-level replay so Build RPC Replay Response always returns authoritative numeric version and non-null status instead of nulls derived from stored_response).
meta.system_prompt_drift_policy: The inline prompt in node 'Build Prompt' is the operational subset of the canonical AGEN-PRMPT-architecture-system-prompt-v1.md. Drift contract: every commit that changes the prompt MUST update both files together; the validator in 'Parse + Validate Architecture' enforces the deterministic invariants (estimated_hours range, DAG, build_order topo sort, component coverage, banned-word regex) the prompt alone cannot.
meta.deployment_note: Per APEX governance, credentials are referenced by ${CRED_*} placeholders in the committed file. The deployment pipeline (apex/spawn_claude.py + the codex-bridge import step) substitutes placeholders → real n8n credential IDs at import time. Concrete IDs live in the apex_governance ops registry, never in version control. The placeholders WILL fail at runtime if the workflow JSON is imported into n8n without substitution. Static reviewers (codex, etc.) may flag this — it is intentional, not a defect.
